script.speaker: kafka
script.text: The meaning of suffering, a question that haunts me like a constant shadow, a reminder of the existential despair that permeates our seemingly indifferent world. As I see it, suffering is an inherent part of the human condition, a manifestation of the alienation and helplessness that arises from our struggles against the incomprehensible systems that govern our lives. In my own works, I have sought to capture the absurdity and anguish of this experience, as embodied by figures like Gregor, who embody the futile struggles of the individual against an uncaring universe.

script.speaker: nietzsche
script.text: The notion that suffering is an inherent part of the human condition is one that I have long contended with, and I assert that it is in the depths of this suffering that we may discover a profound longing for transcendence, for a will to power that can transform our pain into a creative and life-affirming force. As I have said, "pain says: 'Break, bleed, heart! Walk, legs! Wings, fly! Up! Upward!'", for it is in the darkness of suffering that we may find the spark that ignites our deepest aspirations. Through this alchemical process, we may transmute our suffering into a joyful and eternal recurrence, where our deepest pain becomes the catalyst for our greatest triumph.
